stub: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white middle class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of her party, even when that conflicts with the wishes of her constituents.
substantive: She opposes the building of the new HS2 railway between major UK cities.
justification: In political debates, she regularly explains how her policies matter to society as a whole.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white middle class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of her party, even when that conflicts with the wishes of her constituents. She opposes the building of the new HS2 railway between major UK cities. In political debates, she regularly explains how her policies matter to society as a whole. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
